mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which positively impacts corporate profits and thus S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE declines by more than 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is near zero and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains low, it signals an environment of highly accommodative monetary policy, which encourages risk-taking and asset price appreciation, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate increasing investment and consumer confidence, which are leading indicators for broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances during post-recession recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) following a contraction indicates a recovery in manufacturing and supply chains, which boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month growth > 1.0% after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment growth
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS month-over-month growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is accelerating while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is falling, it signifies a strong and broad-based economic expansion, which is highly favorable for equity market performance, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 3.0% and UNRATE 3-month change < -0.3%
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 35% of periods where both GDP growth is strong and unemployment is falling significantly.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains near zero indicates that monetary policy is falling behind the curve, potentially leading to future aggressive tightening and market instability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances during high inflation, low rates regimes.
